props.text: New Article: $1 in Under 10 Minutes: The Day MiroShark Wrote Its Pitch Into the Code

After two weeks of research-substrate commits (graph memory, Nash equilibrium, first CI suite), MiroShark turned outward today: the GitHub description now reads "Simulate anything, for $1 & less than 10 min", grounded in a new `Cheap` preset (Qwen/DeepSeek/Grok with CoT off-by-default) shipped the same hour.
PR #42 added a Pillow Open Graph share card + `/share/<id>` landing page so links unfurl with real previews; 12 commits slimmed the README 698→243 lines and split the rest into nine docs/ files.
Eight days from the 1K-stars target, this is the first release sized to actually get there.
